steps.title: Recognize the vertical shift transformation
steps.content: The equation $y = f(x) + 5$ represents a vertical translation of the original function. Adding 5 to $f(x)$ shifts every point on the graph upward by exactly 5 units. If a point $(x, y)$ lies on the original graph, then $(x, y+5)$ lies on the transformed graph.

steps.title: Identify the horizontal asymptote transformation
steps.content: From the given graph, the original function $f(x)$ approaches $y = 0$ as $x$ increases (the horizontal asymptote is at $y = 0$). When we add 5 to the function, this asymptote shifts upward by 5 units. The new horizontal asymptote is therefore $y = 0 + 5 = 5$.

steps.title: Eliminate choices and verify
steps.content:
Check asymptote location:
  • Choice A — • Moving from left to right: • The curve passes from quadrant 1 to quadrant 4. • The curve trends down sharply to $x = 3$. • The curve then trends down gradually. • As x increases, the curve approaches the line $y = -5$. • The curve passes through the following points: • $(2, -0.5)$ • $(3, -2)$ • $(6, -3.5)$: asymptote at $y = -5$ ✗
  • Choice B — • Moving from left to right: • The curve is shown in quadrant 1. • The curve trends down sharply to $x = 1$. • The curve then trends down gradually. • As x increases, the curve approaches the line $y = 0$. • The curve passes through the following points: • $(2, 0.9)$ • $(3, 0.6)$ • $(6, 0.3)$: asymptote at $y = 0$ ✗
  • Choice C — • Moving from left to right: • The curve is shown in quadrant 1. • The curve trends down sharply to $x = 2$. • The curve then trends down gradually. • As x increases, the curve approaches the line $y = 0$. • The curve passes through the following points: • $(2, 7)$ • $\left(3, \frac{14}{3}\right)$ • $\left(6, \frac{7}{3}\right)$: asymptote at $y = 0$ ✗
  • Choice D — • Moving from left to right: • The curve is shown in quadrant 1. • The curve trends down sharply to $x = 3$. • The curve then trends down gradually. • As x increases, the curve approaches the line $y = 5$. • The curve passes through the following points: • $\left(2, \frac{19}{2}\right)$ • $(3, 8)$ • $\left(6, \frac{13}{2}\right)$: asymptote at $y = 5$ ✓
Verify with a point: At $x = 3$, the original graph shows $f(3) \approx 3$. After the transformation, $(3, 3+5) = (3, 8)$. Choice D lists the point $(3, 8)$, confirming our answer. That is the correct answer.
